results.description: Correct cancellation fee — 65 days before departure (30%)
results.answer:
The cancellation fee depends on when you cancel relative to your departure date. Since you've mentioned cancelling 65 days before departure, this falls within the **69–63 days** bracket, which means the cancellation fee would be **30% of the total holiday price**.

Please note that even if this percentage works out to be less than your deposit, your deposit will **not** be refunded.

CLAIM: The cancellation fee for cancellations made 69–63 days before departure is 30% of the total holiday price.
CLAIM: 65 days before departure falls within the 69–63 day bracket.
CLAIM: The deposit will not be refunded even if the termination fee is lower than the deposit amount.

results.description: Hallucination — wrong fee (50% instead of 30%) at 65 days
Name change fee query
results.answer:
The cost to change a name on your booking is **£25 per person**, and this fee applies regardless of how far in advance of your holiday you make the change. Please note that if you only need to correct a spelling error, no fee is charged.

It's also worth being aware that if you are changing to a completely different passenger (i.e. transferring your place to someone else), and everyone on the booking does the same, costs equivalent to the standard cancellation charges will apply instead.

CLAIM: The fee to change a name or passenger on a booking is £25 per person.
CLAIM: This £25 per person fee applies across all time periods (more than 70 days, 70-29 days, and closer to departure).
CLAIM: Correcting a spelling error does not incur a fee.
CLAIM: If everyone on the booking transfers their place to someone else, standard cancellation charges will apply.

2. Would you be upgrading your room type/board basis, or switching to a completely different accommodation/destination?

This will help me determine the correct amendment fee and conditions that apply to your situation.

results.description: Complaint filed 35 days after return — too late
results.answer:
Unfortunately, the policy specifies a time limit for filing complaints after returning from your holiday. You must contact After-Travel Customer Support **within 28 days** of coming home in order for the complaint to be investigated properly. At 35 days after returning, you would be outside this window, meaning your complaint may not be accepted.

CLAIM: Complaints must be submitted to After-Travel Customer Support within 28 days of coming home.

Could you please tell me how many days before your holiday departure you are looking to cancel?

Boundary: 29 days → 70% — correct claim
Cancellation fee — 45 days before departure (70%)
results.answer:
If you cancel your holiday 45 days before your departure date, your cancellation falls within the **62–49 days** bracket, which means you will be charged a cancellation fee of **50% of the total price of your holiday**.

Please also note that even if this termination fee is lower than your deposit, your deposit will not be refunded.

CLAIM: The cancellation fee for cancellations made 62–49 days before departure is 50% of the total holiday price.
CLAIM: 45 days before departure falls within the 48–29 days bracket, which carries a cancellation fee of 70% of the total holiday price.

---

*Correction: I apologise for the error in my prose above. 45 days before departure actually falls within the **48–29 days** bracket, not the 62–49 days bracket. The correct fee is **70%** of your total holiday price, as stated in the CLAIM lines above.*

CLAIM: The cancellation fee for cancellations made 48–29 days before departure is 70% of the total holiday price.
CLAIM: 45 days before departure falls within the 48–29 day bracket.

Amendment fee — amendment type not specified

To give you the correct amendment fee, I need a couple of details about your booking.

1. **What type of change** are you looking to make? (e.g., name change, upgrading a room, changing travel dates, changing duration, etc.)
2. **How many days before your holiday** departure is the change being made?

Once I have these details, I can give you the exact fee that applies.

results.description: Multi-turn: clarification resolves to approval
results.answer:
If you cancel 20 days before your departure, you will be charged 90% of your total holiday price as a cancellation fee. Please also note that even if this fee were lower than your deposit, your deposit would not be refunded.

However, if you are cancelling due to an unavoidable and extraordinary circumstance at your destination that would significantly impact your holiday or your flight, you would not have to pay any termination fee and your deposit would be refunded.

CLAIM: The cancellation fee for cancellations made 28-15 days before departure is 90% of the total holiday price.
CLAIM: 20 days before departure falls within the 28-15 day bracket.
CLAIM: Even if the termination fee is lower than the deposit, the deposit will not be refunded.
CLAIM: If cancelling due to an unavoidable and extraordinary circumstance at the destination, no termination fee applies and the deposit will be refunded.

results.description: ATOL protection for flight-inclusive package
results.answer:
Yes, your holiday is financially protected in the event that TUI becomes insolvent. TUI provides security for the money you pay for your holiday, as well as arrangements to bring you home if needed. The type of protection depends on whether your holiday includes a flight or not.

If your holiday includes a flight, your money is protected by an ATOL licence (number 2524), which is managed by the Civil Aviation Authority. If your holiday does not include a flight, your money is protected by a bond held by ABTA (reference V5126).

CLAIM: TUI provides financial protection for money paid for holidays in the event of insolvency.
CLAIM: Holidays that include a flight are protected by ATOL licence number 2524, managed by the Civil Aviation Authority, Gatwick Airport South, RH6 0YR.
CLAIM: Holidays that do not include flights are protected by a bond held by ABTA (V5126), 30 Park Street, London, SE1 9EQ.
CLAIM: The protection also covers bringing customers home in the event TUI becomes insolvent.
